scenario: Ridge Insurance models a Camunda 8.8 claim review process where 1500 claims per day might exceed agreed handling times. The throw side wants to compute the escalationCode at runtime from a process variable, while the catch side needs deterministic routing inside the same parent flow scope.
question: Which escalationCode configuration is allowed for escalation events in this Camunda 8.8 model?

options.text: A FEEL expression for escalationCode on both the throw event and on every catching boundary event so the codes can vary per process instance at runtime in the same scope
A static escalationCode on the throw event together with a FEEL expression for escalationCode on the catching boundary event in the same parent flow scope of the process
A FEEL expression for escalationCode on the throw event together with a static escalationCode on the catching boundary event in the same parent flow scope of the process
A FEEL expression for escalationCode on the throw event together with multiple catch-all escalation boundary events on the same parent flow scope of the process at runtime

a.text: Incorrect. The Defining an escalation section states that for escalation catch events escalationCode must be a static value, so a FEEL expression on the catch event is not allowed in this scope.

b.text: Incorrect. The page only allows static values on catch events; a FEEL expression on the catch side of escalationCode is not supported in any escalation catch event configuration.

c.text: Correct. The Defining an escalation section states that throw events may define escalationCode as an expression or a static value, while catch events must use a static value, so this combination is documented and allowed.

d.text: Incorrect. The escalation events page states that multiple catch-all escalation events in a single scope are not permitted, regardless of how escalationCode is defined on the throw side of the event.

explanation: An escalation event needs an escalationCode. Throw events may set it from a FEEL expression or a static value, while catch events require a static value. A catch event may omit the escalation reference entirely to catch every escalation in its scope.
